title: Outcomes tracking (partner self-report) + re-review/freshness cadence + regulatory-update process
context: Because the project collects no telemetry, outcomes are tracked via partner self-report using a standard template. The re-review cadence and regulatory-update process are critical for a medium-risk public-health project: the 2024 PFAS MCLs and the evolving Lead and Copper Rule are explicit re-review triggers. This task operationalizes the ongoing maintenance processes that keep the project honest and accurate after the M3 launch.
objective: Design and document the ongoing operations processes: (1) privacy-preserving outcomes tracking via partner self-report using a standard template (distinct users, de-dup, flagged estimates; no in-app telemetry); (2) content re-review cadence with a named owner; (3) regulatory-update trigger process for PFAS/LCR and any future rule changes, with a named owner and a tested end-to-end workflow; (4) data freshness re-ingest cadence and staleness escalation process.
acceptanceCriteria: Partner self-report template documented: partner, period, channels, distinct users (confirmed), flagged estimates, region/languages, de-dup notes, outcomes note
Re-review cadence documented with a named owner and schedule
Regulatory-update trigger process documented for PFAS/LCR rule changes: how a new rule triggers a content re-review, who owns it, how it is tracked
Data freshness re-ingest cadence and staleness escalation documented
No in-app telemetry; outcomes are partner-reported only, not measured in-app
output: Document: outcomes tracking template, re-review cadence, regulatory-update trigger process, and freshness cadence committed to the repo.
